problem_statement: Vibe-coded projects where developers accept AI-generated code without review introduce operational/packaging security failures (like source-map leaks) that current static-analysis and secret-scanning tools do not detect, leaving the build-to-publish artifact boundary unguarded.
magnitude_evidence: Claude Code npm package shipped a 59.8 MB source map exposing ~1,900 files and 512,000 lines of proprietary TypeScript; CodeRabbit found AI code produces ~1.7x more issues (2.74x for XSS); Veracode reported 45% of LLM code across 80 tasks contained security flaws
insufficiency_evidence: Existing static-analysis and secret-scanning tools did not cover this failure mode; SAST (Semgrep, CodeQL) ignore packaging/artifact content, secret scanners have no model of what appears in a published package, dependency auditors do not inspect the artifact itself; a pre-commit hook would not have caught it because the source map was a legitimate build output
